tipping_trajectory_assessments.reasoning: Three Tier 2 studies (Science Advances x2, Nature Comm. Earth & Env.) provide multi-method confirmation of AMOC weakening: meridional decline in western boundary transport, 51% projected slowdown by 2100, Gulf Stream northward shift as early warning (stat sig trends). Carbon Brief synthesis confirms bistability consensus. Tier 2 evidence alone limits to Assessed acceleration but strongest signals this week.

analyst_briefing: AMOC dominates signals with three Tier 2 studies confirming weakening trajectory: meridional western boundary decline (Science Advances), 51% slowdown projection to 2100, Gulf Stream northward shift as early warning (p<0.05 trend). Assess as trajectory_acceleration (Assessed confidence; Tier 1 needed for Confirmed). No signals across other tipping elements. Planetary boundaries: climate_change reinforced beyond by Copernicus Tier 1 1.5°C three-year exceedance (first occurrence); biosphere_integrity continuing via Europe biodiversity impacts; freshwater_use stressed by El Niño drought forecast. No status changes recommended. Active cascades escalate: El Niño food_insecurity (Asia/C.America/S.America → scem HIGH); Europe infrastructure_failure (heatwaves → gmm MEDIUM). Cross-monitor: scem AMOC/El Niño conflict nexus (HIGH); gmm commodity/asset risks (MEDIUM); wdm governance lag (MEDIUM). Contested: AMOC acceleration signals vs timing uncertainty (hold). Flag AMOC for trajectory acceleration attention.
